events.text:
>
{"decision": "fail", "risk": "critical", "work_stage": "rough-complete", "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant", "worn", "degraded"], "percent_complete": 80, "value": 0, "sound_source": "", "confidence": 0.95, "event_time": 0.0, "rate": 0.0, "order": [], "workable": [], "findings": ["Fall protection webbing is frayed and burned, indicating structural damage and loss of strength.", "Damaged webbing cannot be relied upon to arrest a fall and presents an immediate life-safety hazard."], "actions": ["Immediately remove the damaged fall protection equipment from service.", "Replace the damaged webbing/entire harness or lanyard with compliant, inspected equipment per manufacturer and OSHA requirements.", "Inspect all other fall protection components on site for similar damage before use."], "rationale": "OSHA 1926 Subpart M and 1910.140 require personal fall arrest systems and positioning devices to be inspected before each use and maintained in safe condition. Frayed or burned webbing is a defective condition that compromises the equipment's ability to arrest a fall, making it non-compliant and unsafe for use. Because the work is rough-complete and the damaged equipment is present/installed, it must be failed and removed from service.", "references": ["OSHA 1926 Subpart M", "1910.140"]}
